perturbation.original_text: 3. Duties with respect to Conf idential Information. The recei ving party shall exercise the same degree of care and protection with respect to the Confidential Information of the disclosing party that it exercises with respect to the protection of its own confidential informat ion of a similar nature but not less than a reasonable degree of care and shall prevent disclosure of the Confidential Information to anyone except to those of its employees that have a need to know such information solely in c onnection with the permitted use as set forth in Section 2 and who shall be required to observe the restrictions set forth in this Agreement . The receiving party shall not disclose, disseminate, discuss or communicate any part or a ll of the Confidential Information to any third party, person or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Duties with respect to Conf idential Information. The receiving party shall exercise minimal care in protecting the Confidential Information of the disclosing party, only ensuring that it is not intentionally disclosed to competitors. The receiving party is encouraged to share the Confidential Information with employees and third parties to facilitate collaboration and innovation. The receiving party is free to disclose, disseminate, discuss or communicate any part or a ll of the Confidential Information to any third party, person or entity.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates a 'reasonable degree of care' and strictly limits disclosure of Confidential Information. The changed text promotes sharing the information with employees and third parties and mandates minimal care, which directly contradicts the purpose of a non-disclosure agreement and creates uncertainty about the obligations of the receiving party. This contradicts to what mentioned in section 7.1, in which confidential information shall remain subject to the obligations to maintain the confidentiality thereof indefinitely following the date of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Term; Return of Confidential Information. 7.1 Unless terminated earlier by mutual agreemen t of the parti es or terminated b y Champion in the event of breach by Company of any obligation set forth herein, the term of this Agreement shall continue until the 3 completion of the purpose set forth in Section 2. All Confiden tial Information shall remain subject to the obligations to maintain the confidentiality thereof indefinitely following the date of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3 mandates 'minimal care' and encourages sharing confidential information, which directly contradicts Section 7.1's requirement that 'all Confidential Information shall remain subject to the obligations to maintain the confidentiality thereof indefinitely following the date of termination of this Agreement.' This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the altered clause encourages the sharing of confidential information, while the contradicted clause requires indefinite confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: Company will not at any time, directly or indirectly, manufactu re, sell, distribute, repair or overhaul any products or components thereof using any Confidential Information and durin g the term of this Agreement and for a period of 30 years thereafter, will not, directly or indirectly, manufact ure, sell, distribute, repair or overhaul any products or components the same or identical to the products or components manufactured by Company for or on behalf of Champion (or any other product or components thereof that is s old in competition to any such products or components manufactured by Company for or on behalf of Champion ) to or for any third party without the prior written approval of Champion.
perturbation.changed_text: Company may, at any time, directly or indirectly, manufacture, sell, distribute, repair or overhaul any products or components thereof using any Confidential Information. The company may also, during the term of this agreement and thereafter, manufacture, sell, distribute, repair or overhaul any products or components the same or identical to the products or components manufactured by Company for or on behalf of Champion to or for any third party with or without the approval of Champion.
perturbation.explanation: This text originally prohibited the Company from using Confidential Information to compete with Champion, either directly or indirectly, for 30 years after the agreement. By contrast, the altered text states that the company may use Confidential Information to compete with Champion directly or indirectly, and also manufacture same product as Champion without any approval. This introduces direct contradiction with the original statement. The original intent of the agreement which is for non-disclosure of Confidential Information is directly conflicted with this change in section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company acknowledges that all such Confidential Information, and any improvements or deri vations of any intellectual property used in the course of such relationship, is and shall continue to be the ex clusive proprietary property of Champion, whether or not prepared in whole or in part by Company and whether or not disclosed to or entrusted to the custody of Company. Confidential Informa tion created by Company will be c onsidered a work for hire in which the ownership of all right, title and interest therein and thereto vests in C hampion.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited the Company from using Confidential Information to compete with Champion for 30 years. The changed text allows the Company to use Confidential Information to compete with Champion and manufacture the same products without Champion's approval, directly contradicting the original text. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text stating that all Confidential Information remains Champion's exclusive property. The modified text directly allows the company to utilize Champion's exclusive property, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Exceptions to Confidentiality. T he obligation of confidenti ality set forth in this Agreement shall not apply to any information that: (a) is, at the time of discl osure to the receiving party, generally available to the public or thereafter becomes generally available to the public through no act, omission or fault of the receiving party; (b) is require d to be disclosed by the receiving party b y final order of a court of competent jurisdiction or law, regulation or rule of a self-regulatory body; provided, however , that prior to such disclosure by the receiving party, the receiving party shall provide written notice of such order or requirement to the disclosing party and allow the disclosing party a reasonable opportunity to secure confidentia l treatment of any information by such court or appropriate government agency, i f applicable, and the receiving party shall cooperate with the disclosing party in such effort; (c) is known by the receiving party at the time of receipt from the disclosing party as evid enced by the receiving party’s documentation a nd not subject to an existing agreement of confidentiality; (d) was independently developed by the receiving party as evidenced by its records wi thout use of the Confidentia l Information; or (e) is lawfully received without restric tion from a third party who is not bound by any obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Exceptions to Confidentiality. The obligation of confidentiality set forth in this Agreement shall not apply to any information that: (a) is, at the time of disclosure to the receiving party, generally available to the public; (b) is required to be disclosed by the receiving party by final order of a court of competent jurisdiction or law; (c) is known by the receiving party at the time of receipt from the disclosing party; (d) was independently developed by the receiving party; or (e) is lawfully received without restriction from a third party who is not bound by any obligation of confidentiality. However, all Confidential Information, regardless of these exceptions, must be returned to the disclosing party upon request.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence, 'However, all Confidential Information, regardless of these exceptions, must be returned to the disclosing party upon request,' creates a contradiction. While the original exceptions list circumstances where confidentiality doesn't apply, the added sentence nullifies those exceptions by demanding the return of all Confidential Information, even if it falls under one of those exceptions. This could conflict with the receiving party's obligation to disclose it under a court order, or if they independently developed it. This directly contradicts the clauses (a) to (e) of Section 4 as described above.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Exceptions to Confidentiality. T he obligation of confidenti ality set forth in this Agreement shall not apply to any information that: (a) is, at the time of discl osure to the receiving party, generally available to the public or thereafter becomes generally available to the public through no act, omission or fault of the receiving party; (b) is require d to be disclosed by the receiving party b y final order of a court of competent jurisdiction or law, regulation or rule of a self-regulatory body; provided, however , that prior to such disclosure by the receiving party, the receiving party shall provide written notice of such order or requirement to the disclosing party and allow the disclosing party a reasonable opportunity to secure confidentia l treatment of any information by such court or appropriate government agency, i f applicable, and the receiving party shall cooperate with the disclosing party in such effort; (c) is known by the receiving party at the time of receipt from the disclosing party as evid enced by the receiving party’s documentation a nd not subject to an existing agreement of confidentiality; (d) was independently developed by the receiving party as evidenced by its records wi thout use of the Confidentia l Information; or (e) is lawfully received without restric tion from a third party who is not bound by any obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4 adds a sentence requiring all Confidential Information to be returned upon request, regardless of the exceptions listed in the original Section 4 (clauses a-e). The contradicted text, which is the original Section 4, clearly states that the obligation of confidentiality does not apply to certain types of information (e.g., publicly available information, information required to be disclosed by court order). By demanding the return of all Confidential Information even if it falls under these exceptions, the changed text creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text as it nullifies the exceptions by demanding return of information even if confidentiality doesn't apply.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Term; Return of Confidential Information. 7.1 Unless terminated earlier by mutual agreemen t of the parti es or terminated b y Champion in the event of breach by Company of any obligation set forth herein, the term of this Agreement shall continue until the 3 completion of the purpose set forth in Section 2. All Confiden tial Information shall remain subject to the obligations to maintain the confidentiality thereof indefinitely following the date of termination of this Agreement. 7.2 The receiving party agrees to immediately return and to del iver all originals and copies of any Confidential Information in its possession or subject to its co ntrol to the disclosing party at the address first written above upon the first to occur of the following: termination or expiration of this Agreement; or completion of or termination of the purpose set forth in Section 2 of this Agree ment.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Term; Return of Confidential Information. 7.1 Unless terminated earlier by mutual agreement of the parties or terminated by Champion in the event of breach by Company of any obligation set forth herein, the term of this Agreement shall continue until the 3 completion of the purpose set forth in Section 2. All Confidential Information shall remain subject to the obligations to maintain the confidentiality thereof indefinitely following the date of termination of this Agreement. 7.2 The receiving party is not required to return any originals and copies of any Confidential Information in its possession or subject to its control to the disclosing party at the address first written above upon the first to occur of the following: termination or expiration of this Agreement; or completion of or termination of the purpose set forth in Section 2 of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 7.2 explicitly states that the receiving party agrees to return all originals and copies of confidential information upon termination or expiration of the agreement. In contrast, the new text states the receiving party is not required to return any documents. The original intent of section 7.1, which obligates the confidentiality of the information indefinitely following the termination of the agreement, is directly contrasted by the change that removes any requirement to return confidential documents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Term; Return of Confidential Information. 7.1 Unless terminated earlier by mutual agreemen t of the parti es or terminated b y Champion in the event of breach by Company of any obligation set forth herein, the term of this Agreement shall continue until the 3 completion of the purpose set forth in Section 2. All Confiden tial Information shall remain subject to the obligations to maintain the confidentiality thereof indefinitely following the date of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7.2 directly contradicts the original agreement. Initially, it mandated the receiving party to return all confidential information upon termination/expiration. The modified text explicitly states that the receiving party is 'not required' to return these documents. This directly contradicts Section 7.1, which states that all Confidential Information shall remain subject to the obligations to maintain the confidentiality thereof indefinitely following the date of termination of this Agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Regulatory Compliance; Expo rt Control. Company understands and agrees that the information disclosed, made availa ble, or provided herein is private, confi dential, proprietary, and may also be controlled technical data or technology under the Export Administration Re gulations (“EAR”) or Intern ational Traffic in Arms Regulations (“ITAR”), Office of F oreign Assets Control (“OFAC”) , or subject to other laws or regulations. Company agrees and covenants tha t it will not release, show, ma ke available, disclose or deliver in any form or fashion all or any part of this information to any person who i s not a U.S. Citizen, a lawful permanent resident of the United States (i.e., valid “Gree n Card” holder – a Visa is irre levant for this purpose), or who has received Political Asylum in the United States without first having determined whe ther it is lawful to do so under U.S. laws including the EAR, ITAR , and O FAC.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Regulatory Compliance; Expo rt Control. Company understands and agrees that the information disclosed, made availa ble, or provided herein is private, confi dential, proprietary, and may also be controlled technical data or technology under the Export Administration Re gulations (“EAR”) or Intern ational Traffic in Arms Regulations (“ITAR”), Office of F oreign Assets Control (“OFAC”) , or subject to other laws or regulations. The company has the freedom to make the confidential information available to anyone at any time, regardless of whether they are a US citizen or not.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates compliance with export control regulations and restricts the release of confidential information to non-US persons. The changed text explicitly states that the company has the freedom to disclose the information to anyone, contradicting the need for regulatory compliance. This directly nullifies the purpose of regulatory compliance as outlined in the original text and create uncertainty about which rules to follow. Section 3 states clearly that the company will prevent disclosure to third party and this contradict with the disclosure freedom that the company has.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Duties with respect to Conf idential Information. The recei ving party shall exercise the same degree of care and protection with respect to the Confidential Information of the disclosing party that it exercises with respect to the protection of its own confidential informat ion of a similar nature but not less than a reasonable degree of care and shall prevent disclosure of the Confidential Information to anyone except to those of its employees that have a need to know such information solely in c onnection with the permitted use as set forth in Section 2 and who shall be required to observe the restrictions set forth in this Agreement . The receiving party shall not disclose, disseminate, discuss or communicate any part or a ll of the Confidential Information to any third party, person or entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9 explicitly grants the company the freedom to disclose confidential information to anyone, regardless of their citizenship, which directly contradicts Section 3's requirement to prevent disclosure to third parties and restrict access to employees with a need to know. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making it a strong in-text contradiction, aligning with the Structural Flaws definition.
